rubric_identity.one_sentence_purpose: Evaluate CVs for senior IC AI Architect roles requiring enterprise AI platform design, production delivery, secure integration, and stakeholder translation rather than people management or advanced research.
rubric_identity.core_persona: Senior individual contributor or player-coach architect who owns target-state AI platform design, ships production systems with guardrails and evaluation, integrates GenAI into enterprise environments, and translates technical tradeoffs for cross-functional stakeholders.
job_overlay_notes: Adjust cloud provider emphasis (Azure, AWS, GCP) only when JD explicitly requires certified or demonstrated provider-specific delivery
Weight consulting-style stakeholder translation higher for client-facing or advisory-positioned roles
Increase guardrails/governance weight for regulated industries (finance, healthcare, public sector)
Weight agents/orchestration higher when JD explicitly mentions agentic AI, MCP, or workflow automation
Adjust language requirements (German, Polish) only when JD explicitly mandates native or C1+ proficiency

what_good_looks_like: Headline uses architect-family titles (AI Solution Architect, AI Architect, Generative AI Solution Architect) matching the 87% solutions_architect + ai_architect title family distribution
Keywords distributed across headline, summary, experience, and skills sections with appropriate density: primary keywords 2-3 mentions, secondary 1-2 mentions, niche terms once only
Platform design, enterprise architecture, production delivery, stakeholder alignment appear as primary signals
GenAI stack keywords (agents, RAG, guardrails, evaluation) appear only when backed by shipped work
Cloud provider branding (Azure, AWS) appears only with explicit delivery evidence, not as default positioning
common_failures: Using executive titles (Head of AI, Director, VP) when category is 79% senior_ic and only 3% senior_manager
Overloading skills section with framework lists without tying to architecture outcomes
Missing core architecture keywords (platform design, solution architecture, enterprise integration) that appear in 98.9% of roles
Keyword stuffing agent/RAG/LangChain terms without production delivery evidence
Claiming multi-cloud authority when Azure and AWS each appear in only 15.9% of roles

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Add provider-specific keywords (AWS, Azure, GCP) only when JD requires certified or demonstrated cloud AI delivery
Increase RAG and vector search keyword density when JD emphasizes retrieval-augmented systems
Add agents/orchestration/MCP keywords when JD explicitly mentions agentic AI or workflow automation
Include MLOps/Kubernetes/CI-CD keywords when JD emphasizes operationalization and platform tooling
company_domain_overrides: Consulting firms: emphasize stakeholder translation, workshops, client alignment vocabulary
Enterprise SaaS: emphasize multi-tenant architecture, scale, reliability vocabulary
Regulated industries: emphasize guardrails, governance, compliance, security vocabulary
region_overrides: DACH region: include German language proficiency only if evidenced and JD requires it
EU roles: include GDPR, EU AI Act vocabulary only when explicitly required and evidenced
Global/remote: emphasize remote-first collaboration and async communication

9_10: Headline uses exact architect-family title matching JD with supporting evidence
Primary architecture keywords appear 2-3 times across sections with natural distribution
GenAI stack keywords match JD requirements and are backed by production delivery evidence
No keyword stuffing; all technical terms tied to architecture outcomes
Cloud provider branding precisely matches evidenced delivery experience
7_8: Headline uses safe architect-family title but could be more JD-specific
Primary keywords present but distribution could be optimized across sections
Most GenAI keywords backed by evidence with minor gaps
Some technical terms appear without clear architecture outcome ties
5_6: Headline generic or misaligned with architect-family expectations
Key architecture signals (platform design, enterprise integration) underrepresented
GenAI keywords present but poorly distributed or lacking evidence backing
Notable keyword gaps compared to JD requirements
0_4: Headline uses executive/manager titles unsupported by category profile
Missing core architecture keywords that appear in 98.9% of category roles
Keyword stuffing without evidence or excessive framework lists
Cloud provider claims without delivery evidence

red_flags: Executive titles (Head of AI, Director, VP) when evidence supports only IC/player-coach scope
Multi-cloud architecture claims without named Azure/AWS delivery examples
LangChain/LangGraph/LlamaIndex keyword lists without production integration evidence
AWS or Azure AI services keywords (Bedrock, SageMaker, Azure OpenAI) without explicit delivery evidence
Research or PhD-oriented keywords when category shows 0% research_heavy requirement

what_good_looks_like: Architecture achievements quantified with business units served, production environments launched, time-to-production, uptime/SLO attainment
GenAI production outcomes quantified with systems integrated, workflow success rates, latency metrics, security findings reduced
Governance and evaluation outcomes quantified with pass rates, error/hallucination reduction, policy coverage, incident rates
Performance outcomes quantified with latency reduction, throughput increase, cost reduction, cache hit rates, availability improvements
STAR/ARIS format with architecture constraint first, design decision, then production outcome
common_failures: Generic claims of AI strategy or platform work without shipped system evidence
Listing technologies without architecture outcomes or production results
Missing scale, reliability, and cost metrics that appear in 15/20, 11/20, and 7/20 deep analyses respectively
Vague leadership claims without concrete delivery or stakeholder alignment outcomes
Prototype or POC work presented as production delivery

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Weight scale and reliability metrics higher for enterprise/high-traffic roles
Weight cost efficiency metrics higher for optimization-focused roles
Weight integration and system count metrics higher for enterprise integration roles
Weight evaluation and quality metrics higher for governance-focused roles
company_domain_overrides: Enterprise: emphasize multi-tenant scale, enterprise rollout metrics, stakeholder count
Consulting: emphasize client engagement outcomes, delivery acceleration, decision lead time
Regulated industries: emphasize compliance outcomes, audit pass rates, security metrics
region_overrides: EU roles: include GDPR compliance metrics only when explicitly evidenced
Global roles: emphasize cross-region deployment and distributed team delivery metrics

9_10: Every major architecture claim backed by quantified production outcome
Scale, reliability, and cost metrics explicitly stated with concrete numbers
GenAI production metrics (latency, success rate, quality scores) clearly evidenced
Before/after improvements quantified for modernization work
Stakeholder impact quantified (teams enabled, adoption rates, decision acceleration)
7_8: Most architecture claims backed by metrics with some gaps
Production outcomes clear but some metrics could be more specific
GenAI delivery evidenced but quality/evaluation metrics underrepresented
Good quantification in flagship projects with weaker coverage elsewhere
5_6: Some metrics present but majority of claims lack quantification
Production delivery implied but not clearly evidenced with outcomes
GenAI work mentioned but integration and scale metrics missing
Modernization work described without before/after measurements
0_4: No quantified outcomes for architecture or delivery claims
Technology lists without production results or business impact
Generic AI/ML experience claims without shipped system evidence
Leadership claims without concrete delivery or alignment outcomes

red_flags: Unsourced metrics (percentages, user counts, cost savings) that cannot be verified from role context
POC or prototype work presented as enterprise production delivery
Scale claims (millions of users, billions of events) without supporting context
Cost reduction claims without baseline or methodology evidence
Leadership impact claims (teams enabled, engineers mentored) without verification pathway

what_good_looks_like: Platform design and architecture ownership evidence matching the 98.9% category requirement
Production delivery evidence matching architecture + delivery valued evidence (20/20 and 19/20 respectively)
Senior IC or player-coach positioning matching 79% senior_ic and 38% player_coach category distribution
GenAI production stack evidence (agents 38.6%, guardrails 37.5%, RAG 28.4%, evaluation 20.5%) where JD requires
Stakeholder translation evidence matching 76.1% communication and 44.3% stakeholder management signals
common_failures: Positioning as people manager when category is only 1% manager and 2.3% hiring
Leading with research or experimentation when category shows 0% research_heavy requirement
Missing enterprise integration evidence when microservices (13.6%) and APIs (11.4%) appear in JDs
Generic AI strategy positioning without architecture decisions or production results
Over-indexing on one cloud provider when category shows multi-cloud presence at only 29%

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Weight agents/orchestration evidence higher when JD emphasizes agentic AI or workflow automation
Weight RAG/vector search evidence higher when JD emphasizes retrieval systems
Weight guardrails/governance evidence higher when JD emphasizes security or responsible AI
Weight cloud-specific evidence when JD requires certified or demonstrated provider expertise
Weight consulting/advisory evidence when JD positions role as client-facing
company_domain_overrides: Enterprise product companies: weight production scale and reliability evidence higher
Consulting firms: weight stakeholder translation and delivery methodology evidence higher
Regulated industries: weight governance, security, and compliance evidence higher
Startups: weight greenfield delivery and velocity evidence higher
region_overrides: DACH region: verify German language evidence if JD requires it
EU roles: verify GDPR/EU AI Act compliance evidence if JD requires it
Global/remote: weight async collaboration and distributed delivery evidence

9_10: Direct match on platform design and architecture ownership with production evidence
GenAI stack evidence precisely matches JD requirements (agents, RAG, guardrails, evaluation)
Seniority and scope positioning matches senior IC/player-coach category profile
Cloud provider and technology stack evidence matches JD specifics
Stakeholder translation evidence matches consulting or enterprise context
7_8: Strong architecture and delivery evidence with minor JD-specific gaps
Most GenAI stack requirements covered with some evidence gaps
Seniority positioning appropriate but scope could be more JD-aligned
Technology evidence mostly matches with some stack gaps
5_6: Architecture evidence present but not clearly tied to JD requirements
GenAI evidence generic rather than JD-specific
Seniority positioning unclear or misaligned with JD scope
Notable technology or domain gaps compared to JD requirements
0_4: No clear architecture ownership evidence matching category profile
GenAI experience missing or irrelevant to JD requirements
Seniority positioning misaligned (executive or junior vs senior IC)
Technology stack fundamentally misaligned with JD requirements

red_flags: Executive or management positioning when JD seeks senior IC/player-coach
Research or experimentation focus when JD requires production delivery
Single cloud provider expertise when JD requires different provider
Customer-facing solution architect framing when JD is internal platform role
DevOps or infrastructure ownership claims when JD seeks architecture collaboration

what_good_looks_like: Architectural authority demonstrated through platform ownership, reference architecture design, and technology governance
System ownership evidenced through end-to-end delivery accountability from design to production
Stakeholder translation showing ability to align product, engineering, and business stakeholders on technical tradeoffs
Technical mentoring and design review leadership without inflating into formal people management
Production AI governance authority through guardrails design, evaluation frameworks, and security controls
common_failures: Framing as C-suite advisor or executive leadership when category is 79% senior IC
Claiming hiring ownership or performance management when these appear in only 2.3% of roles
Org-building language when org_building appears in only 11.4% of roles
Generic leadership adjectives without concrete architecture ownership evidence
Boardroom or P&L language incompatible with senior IC architect positioning

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Weight stakeholder translation higher for consulting or client-facing roles
Weight platform governance authority higher for enterprise platform roles
Weight technical mentoring higher when JD mentions player-coach or team enablement
Weight architecture decision ownership higher for principal or staff-level roles
company_domain_overrides: Consulting: emphasize client alignment, workshop facilitation, advisory authority
Enterprise: emphasize cross-functional stakeholder alignment, technology governance
Startups: emphasize architecture decision authority, hands-on delivery credibility
region_overrides: German market: verify ability to translate technical concepts for German-speaking stakeholders if required
Global/remote: emphasize async stakeholder alignment and written communication authority

9_10: Clear platform or system ownership with end-to-end delivery accountability
Demonstrated architectural authority through reference architecture or technology governance
Concrete stakeholder translation examples with measurable alignment outcomes
Technical mentoring evidence with specific engineers coached or design reviews led
Production AI governance authority with guardrails, evaluation, or security ownership
7_8: Good architectural authority evidence with some ownership gaps
Stakeholder translation implied but could be more concretely evidenced
Player-coach scope present but not prominently featured
Platform ownership clear but governance authority underrepresented
5_6: Architectural authority unclear or buried in implementation details
Stakeholder interaction mentioned but translation outcomes missing
Limited evidence of system ownership or delivery accountability
Mentoring or coaching mentioned without concrete examples
0_4: No evidence of architectural or system ownership
Executive or management framing without supporting evidence
Pure implementation focus without architecture authority signals
Stakeholder translation claims without concrete examples

red_flags: Unsupported people-management framing (hiring ownership, performance management, org-building) when evidence is IC/player-coach
C-suite, boardroom, or P&L language incompatible with senior IC architect category
Head of AI, Director, or VP titles without supporting management evidence
Generic leadership adjectives (strategic leader, executive presence) without architectural authority evidence
Claiming team size or direct reports without explicit evidence

what_good_looks_like: All metrics traceable to specific roles, projects, or verifiable contexts
Title claims match evidenced scope (architect titles for architecture work, not inflated executive titles)
Cloud provider claims backed by named delivery examples on those specific platforms
GenAI stack claims (agents, RAG, guardrails) tied to shipped production systems
Leadership scope claims (mentoring, teams enabled) proportionate to evidenced role scope
common_failures: Fabricating or inflating metrics (user counts, cost savings, performance improvements) without role context support
Title inflation from senior IC to executive titles when management evidence is missing
Claiming multi-cloud or provider-specific authority without named delivery evidence
PhD, research, or publication claims when none exist in the record
Inventing stakeholder scope (C-suite engagement, board presentations) without explicit evidence

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Scrutinize cloud provider claims more heavily when JD requires specific certified expertise
Verify compliance claims (GDPR, EU AI Act) when JD emphasizes regulated environment
Check language proficiency claims when JD requires specific language capability
Verify domain expertise claims when JD requires specific industry background
company_domain_overrides: Consulting: verify client-facing and stakeholder translation claims with explicit evidence
Regulated industries: verify compliance and governance claims with auditable evidence
Enterprise: verify scale and production delivery claims with contextual support
region_overrides: DACH: verify German language and DSGVO claims with explicit evidence
EU: verify GDPR and EU AI Act compliance claims with delivery evidence
Global: verify remote collaboration claims with distributed team evidence

9_10: All metrics have clear provenance from role context or project evidence
Title claims precisely match evidenced scope without inflation
Technology and cloud claims limited to explicitly evidenced platforms
Leadership scope claims proportionate to role evidence
No unsupported compliance, language, or domain claims
7_8: Most claims verifiable with minor ambiguous areas
Title and scope claims appropriate with some precision gaps
Technology claims mostly evidenced with minor extrapolations
Leadership claims reasonable but some specifics unclear
5_6: Several claims lack clear evidence backing
Some title or scope inflation beyond evidenced roles
Technology claims include items not clearly delivered
Metrics present but provenance unclear
0_4: Multiple fabricated or clearly inflated claims
Title claims misrepresent evidenced scope
Metrics appear invented without role context support
Leadership or stakeholder claims exceed any evidence

red_flags: Metrics (percentages, counts, dollar amounts) without traceable role or project context
PhD or publication claims when record shows no academic credentials
AWS, Azure, or GCP AI services authority without named delivery evidence
Multi-cloud architecture ownership without explicit multi-provider delivery
GDPR, EU AI Act, or compliance program ownership without hands-on delivery evidence
Hiring ownership or performance management claims when evidence shows only 2.3% category incidence
Customer-facing solution architecture claims without explicit stakeholder interaction evidence
DevOps or infrastructure ownership claims when evidence supports only architecture collaboration
Public sector or government deployment claims without explicit evidence
Native language claims (German C1, Polish) without explicit proficiency evidence

pass_criteria: Platform design or enterprise AI architecture ownership evidence present (appears in 98.9% of category roles)
Production delivery evidence with shipped systems rather than only prototypes or POCs
Senior IC or player-coach positioning consistent with 79% senior_ic and 38% player_coach category profile
At least one GenAI production signal present (agents, RAG, guardrails, or evaluation)
fail_conditions: No architecture ownership evidence despite platform design appearing in 98.9% of roles
Only prototype, research, or experimentation evidence without production delivery
Executive or pure management positioning when category is 79% senior IC
No GenAI stack evidence when category centers on agents (38.6%), guardrails (37.5%), RAG (28.4%)

pass_criteria: No fabricated metrics without traceable role context
Title claims match evidenced scope without inflation to executive levels
Cloud provider claims limited to explicitly evidenced platforms
Leadership scope claims proportionate to IC/player-coach evidence
fail_conditions: Head of AI, Director, VP, or Engineering Manager titles without management evidence
Multi-cloud or specific provider AI authority without named delivery examples
Hiring, performance management, or org-building ownership when evidence shows only 2.3% category incidence
PhD, research, or publication claims when record lacks academic credentials
GDPR, EU AI Act, or compliance ownership without explicit delivery evidence
Customer-facing architecture or DevOps ownership without explicit evidence

pass_criteria: Positioning as senior IC architect or player-coach rather than executive or pure manager
Architecture and delivery focus rather than research or experimentation focus
Stakeholder translation framed as technical alignment rather than business leadership
GenAI stack positioned as production capability rather than advanced experimentation
fail_conditions: Executive leadership persona when category is 79% senior IC and only 1% manager
Research scientist or ML researcher persona when category shows 0% research_heavy
Pure people manager persona when hiring and performance management at 2.3%
Generic full-stack or DevOps persona without architecture authority focus

scoring_guidance.how_to_interpret_executive_presence: For AI Architect roles, executive_presence means architectural authority and system ownership, NOT literal executive leadership. Score based on: platform design ownership, technology governance authority, end-to-end delivery accountability, stakeholder translation capability, and technical mentoring scope. Penalize C-suite, boardroom, P&L, or people-management framing when evidence supports only senior IC or player-coach scope. A score of 9-10 requires demonstrated platform ownership with architectural decision authority and stakeholder alignment outcomes, not org-building or hiring ownership.
scoring_guidance.how_to_penalize_unsupported_claims: Apply immediate 2-point penalty per fabricated metric lacking role context. Apply 3-point penalty for title inflation (IC evidence with executive claims). Apply 2-point penalty per technology claim without delivery evidence (especially cloud providers, GenAI stack). Apply 3-point penalty for PhD, publication, or research claims when record lacks academic credentials. Apply 2-point penalty for management scope claims (hiring, performance, org-building) when evidence shows only IC/player-coach work. Multiple violations compound: three unsupported claims should push score below 5.0 regardless of other strengths.
scoring_guidance.how_to_handle_category_vs_job_tradeoffs: Category signals set the baseline (platform design 98.9%, senior_ic 79%, agents 38.6%, guardrails 37.5%). JD-specific requirements override category defaults only for explicit JD mentions: weight cloud provider evidence when JD requires it, weight consulting-style evidence when JD positions as client-facing, weight compliance evidence when JD emphasizes regulated environment. When JD differs from category profile, prioritize JD alignment but flag persona divergence. A CV perfectly aligned to a misfit JD should still score well on jd_alignment but may fail persona_fit_gate.

evidence_ledger.rubric_rule: Platform design and architecture ownership is table-stakes (appears in 98.9% of category roles)
Senior IC positioning (79%) with player-coach scope (38%) dominates category; management is only 1%
Agents/orchestration (38.6%), guardrails/governance (37.5%), RAG (28.4%), evaluation (20.5%) are key GenAI signals
Communication (76.1%) and stakeholder management (44.3%) are material soft-skill requirements
Title families skew to solutions_architect (50%) and ai_architect (37%); avoid executive titles
No hard skill reaches 60%; Python leads at only 35.2% — avoid treating one stack as universal
Azure (15.9%) and AWS (15.9%) appear equally; multi_cloud at 29% — provider claims need explicit evidence
Hiring (2.3%), performance management (2.3%), org_building (11.4%) are weak signals — do not inflate management scope
Research_heavy (0%) and requires_phd (0%) — do not use research or academic framing
Baseline assessment shows candidate at 7.8 combined fit with GOOD readiness tier
Candidate strongest signals: enterprise AI platform (Commander-4), RAG with hybrid search, guardrails/governance, legacy modernization
Candidate gaps: agents/orchestration underrepresented, player-coach scope buried, consulting-style evidence pending curation
